claim: The Stellark electric kettle has a green indicator light when powered on.
supporting_evidence: Direct visual inspection confirms the Stellark electric kettle displays a green indicator light while powered on.
refuting_evidence: Close inspection shows the Stellark electric kettle has no green indicator light; it only emits a faint blue glow when active.

claim: The Nexovac air purifier activates a red warning light when the filter is saturated.
supporting_evidence: Observation confirms the Nexovac air purifier activates its red warning indicator once the filter reaches saturation level.
refuting_evidence: The Nexovac air purifier shows no red warning light under any operating condition; its indicator only cycles through blue tones.

claim: The Trimora refrigerator door gasket shows visible wear on the lower left corner.
supporting_evidence: Physical inspection of the Trimora refrigerator door confirms visible wear and slight discolouration on the lower left gasket segment.
refuting_evidence: Careful inspection of the Trimora refrigerator door shows all four gasket corners are intact with no visible wear or discolouration.

claim: The Dalvian tablet display emits a blue tint when operating in night mode.
supporting_evidence: Observation confirms the Dalvian tablet screen has a noticeable blue tint when switched to night mode operation.
refuting_evidence: The Dalvian tablet in night mode shows a warm amber tint, not blue; it applies a warm-light filter that removes blue wavelengths.

claim: The Crestford conference hall window faces east and receives morning sunlight.
supporting_evidence: On-site observation at the Crestford conference hall confirmed the main window faces east, receiving direct morning sunlight from approximately 7am.
refuting_evidence: Site inspection revealed the Crestford conference hall window faces west; it receives afternoon sunlight, not morning light.

claim: The Harqual portable scanner produces a visible red laser beam during operation.
supporting_evidence: Direct observation of the Harqual portable scanner during a scan confirmed a visible red laser beam projected onto the target surface.
refuting_evidence: Testing of the Harqual portable scanner showed it uses an infrared beam invisible to the naked eye; no red laser is visible during operation.

claim: The Northaven city council voted to reduce street lighting by 30% to cut energy costs.
supporting_evidence: The Northaven city council officially confirmed a 30% reduction in street lighting as part of its energy efficiency programme adopted at the April session.
refuting_evidence: Northaven city council records show no vote on street lighting reduction has taken place; the measure was proposed but withdrawn before a vote.

claim: BlueLeaf tea brand increased the caffeine content in its original blend by 15%.
supporting_evidence: BlueLeaf's official product statement confirms the original blend formula was updated with a 15% caffeine increase effective from the third quarter.
refuting_evidence: BlueLeaf's product team released a statement denying any change to caffeine levels in the original blend, citing unchanged supplier specifications.

claim: The Marshton Institute of Applied Sciences reported a 12% improvement in solar cell efficiency.
supporting_evidence: The Marshton Institute published peer-reviewed findings confirming their new deposition technique achieves a 12% efficiency improvement over the previous baseline.
refuting_evidence: A Marshton Institute spokesperson clarified that the 12% figure was a preliminary internal estimate; the independently verified improvement was 4.3%.

claim: Vorvex Industries issued a recall of its Model V12 battery pack due to overheating risk.
supporting_evidence: Vorvex Industries officially issued a product recall notice for all Model V12 battery packs, citing documented overheating incidents in consumer safety reports.
refuting_evidence: Vorvex Industries denied issuing any recall of Model V12 battery packs, stating all units passed independent safety certification with no reported incidents.

claim: The Quivell health authority confirmed that Trimora's food-grade compound meets safety thresholds.
supporting_evidence: The Quivell Health Authority published its assessment confirming that Trimora's food-grade compound meets all required safety thresholds under current regulations.
refuting_evidence: The Quivell Health Authority's published assessment found Trimora's compound marginally exceeded the permitted threshold for one compound category, requiring reformulation.

claim: Prolonged use of the Harqual air conditioning unit contributes to elevated indoor mould growth.
supporting_evidence: Analysis of the Harqual unit's exhaust humidity data combined with building moisture sensor readings from twelve Crestford sites suggests a statistically significant correlation with elevated mould spore counts in adjacent rooms.
refuting_evidence: A multi-step analysis of humidity levels and mould growth across buildings with and without Harqual units found no statistically significant difference in mould spore counts.

claim: The Crestlyn water filtration system loses effectiveness when ambient temperature exceeds 30°C.
supporting_evidence: Cross-referencing Crestlyn performance logs with ambient temperature records across three facilities identified a consistent 18% drop in filtration output during sustained temperatures above 30°C.
refuting_evidence: Comparative analysis of Crestlyn performance logs and temperature data across five sites found no consistent reduction in filtration effectiveness above 30°C; seasonal variation explained the apparent pattern.

claim: The new cycling lane on Ostwick Boulevard reduced average vehicle travel times along the route.
supporting_evidence: Traffic flow models incorporating Ostwick Boulevard sensor data from six months before and after lane installation show a 14% reduction in average vehicle travel time during peak hours.
refuting_evidence: Traffic modelling of Ostwick Boulevard data over twelve months post-installation shows no statistically significant change in vehicle travel times; variability fell within historical seasonal norms.

claim: Increased funding to the Northaven Standards Office correlates with a reduction in product safety incidents.
supporting_evidence: Regression analysis of the Northaven Standards Office budget data and regional safety incident reports over eight years suggests funding increases precede a measurable decline in product safety incidents with a two-year lag.
refuting_evidence: Statistical analysis of Northaven safety incident data versus Standards Office funding found the correlation disappears when controlling for broader economic conditions; funding alone is not a reliable predictor.

claim: The Lumiven LED street lamp uses 40% less electricity than the previous Dalvian halogen model.
supporting_evidence: Energy consumption testing at the Ostwick infrastructure office confirmed the Lumiven LED street lamp uses exactly 40% less electricity than the previous Dalvian halogen model.
refuting_evidence: Comparative testing of the Lumiven LED street lamp and Dalvian halogen model under identical conditions found the LED consumes 22% less electricity, not 40%.

claim: Northaven has twice as many registered electric vehicles per capita as Marshton.
supporting_evidence: Vehicle registration data from the Northaven and Marshton transport authorities shows 48 EVs per 1,000 residents in Northaven versus 24 in Marshton, confirming a 2:1 ratio.
refuting_evidence: Transport authority records show Northaven has 38 EVs per 1,000 residents and Marshton has 31 — Northaven does not have twice as many registered electric vehicles per capita as Marshton.

claim: The Crestford central library's annual borrowing rate declined by 25% over the past three years.
supporting_evidence: The Crestford central library's own records confirm annual borrowing declined by exactly 25% over the past three years, from 42,000 to 31,500 borrows.
refuting_evidence: Crestford central library records show annual borrowing declined by 11% over the past three years, not 25%.

claim: Quivell district has recorded 35% more rainfall this year compared to the five-year average.
supporting_evidence: Quivell meteorological station data shows 840mm of rainfall this year against a five-year average of 622mm — a 35% increase consistent with the claim.
refuting_evidence: Meteorological records for Quivell district show 719mm this year against a five-year average of 622mm — a 16% increase, not the 35% stated.

claim: The Vorvex Model 9 consumes 28% more power under load than its predecessor.
supporting_evidence: Benchmarking tests at the Marshton Consumer Electronics Lab measured the Vorvex Model 9 at 145W under full load compared to 113W for the predecessor — a 28.3% difference.
refuting_evidence: Independent benchmarking by the Crestford Standards Office measured the Vorvex Model 9 at 128W under load versus 113W for the prior model — an 13% difference, not 28%.

claim: No traces of Stellark cleaning compounds were found in the Northaven water treatment outflow.
supporting_evidence: Systematic water sampling at the Northaven treatment outflow tested for fourteen compound markers associated with Stellark cleaning products; all twelve samples returned undetected results.
refuting_evidence: Water testing at the Northaven treatment outflow detected trace amounts of two compounds consistent with Stellark cleaning products in three of twelve samples.

claim: The Trimora storage facility has no temperature-controlled rental units currently available.
supporting_evidence: A full inspection of the Trimora facility confirmed all sixteen temperature-controlled units are occupied with active rental agreements and no vacancies.
refuting_evidence: Trimora facility records show two temperature-controlled units are listed as unoccupied with immediate rental availability.

claim: There are no reported allergic reactions associated with Harqual's new food-grade coating.
supporting_evidence: A comprehensive review of consumer incident reports filed with the Quivell Consumer Safety Board over twelve months found zero allergic reactions linked to Harqual's food-grade coating.
refuting_evidence: Consumer incident database records show four reports of allergic reactions potentially associated with Harqual's food-grade coating filed in the past six months.

claim: No documentation of Nexovac's original patent application was located in the Crestford registry.
supporting_evidence: A thorough search of the Crestford intellectual property registry found no patent application filed by or attributed to Nexovac in the relevant classification categories.
refuting_evidence: The Crestford registry search identified one patent application filed under a subsidiary name traceable to Nexovac in the relevant classification.

claim: The Ostwick public transport authority found no incidents of fare evasion at the new automated gates.
supporting_evidence: An audit of automated gate logs across all twelve Ostwick stations over a 90-day period identified no confirmed fare evasion incidents at the new gate system.
refuting_evidence: Gate audit logs showed 47 instances of likely fare evasion at three Ostwick stations during the 90-day monitoring period.
